title: TaskHandoff payload
description: Emitted once per work.lifecycle_step call. The composite event that records a chain-task handoff seam (close T_n + start T_n+1) as one affordance-level fact, in addition to the per-op TaskCompleted + TaskStarted events that the underlying work.batch dispatch produces. Listeners that subscribe to TaskHandoff get the single-edge view; listeners that subscribe to TaskCompleted / TaskStarted continue to receive their per-op events unchanged (the T1 design audit's Decision 2 tightening — composite IN ADDITION TO per-op, not in lieu of). The envelope's entity is the closing task (the chain's perspective: the seam belongs to the task that just finished); the started task lives in payload.started. Both ops MUST belong to the same chain — work.lifecycle_step rejects cross-chain handoffs at the validation gate, so this payload's chain_slug is unambiguous.

chain_slug.description: The chain both ops belong to. Denormalized from both tasks; work.lifecycle_step enforces the same-chain invariant before dispatching the batch.

closed.description: The task that closed in this seam. Mirrors task_complete's params + the cascade event id.

task_slug.description: The slug of the task that closed.

commit_sha.description: The closing commit SHA, or the literal sentinel 'unversioned' for tasks whose artifacts live outside any git repo (matches mcp__toolkit-server__work convention). Optional because the underlying task_complete handler accepts close-without-SHA; lifecycle_step does not tighten that gate.

handoff_output.description: Verbatim handoff field from the closing task. Distinct from rationale — handoff_output is operational color (what artifacts the next task consumes); rationale is the closing 'why this counts as done'.

rationale.description: Per-op rationale supplied to lifecycle_step (close_rationale). Required — every batch sub-op requires per-op rationale, and lifecycle_step inherits that gate.

event_id.description: The cascade TaskCompleted event's id. Present when the underlying work.batch outer tx committed.

started.description: The task that started in this seam. Mirrors task_start's params + the cascade event id.

task_slug.description: The slug of the task that started.

rationale.description: Per-op rationale supplied to lifecycle_step (next_rationale). Required.

event_id.description: The cascade TaskTransitioned (pending→active) event's id. Present when the underlying work.batch outer tx committed.
